description: The Telegram coding-intent refactor is initialized as a standalone harness separate from the completed auto-launch task.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the Telegram coding-intent refactor.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the current parser limitations and the new intent-driven scope.
Confirm the task scope is limited to Telegram private-chat coding-task detection and repo resolution.

description: Configuration can provide a repo alias table for coding tasks, and the runtime wires that alias map into the Telegram coding-task entrypoint.
steps: Load config with a coding-task repo alias map.
Verify the active coding-task runtime receives the alias map without changing CLI coding-task behavior.
Confirm the gateway path still boots with no alias map configured.

description: A dedicated repo resolver resolves repo refs by checking configured aliases first and then falling back to ~/Documents/<repo>.
steps: Resolve a configured alias and verify it returns the configured absolute path.
Resolve an unconfigured repo name that exists under ~/Documents and verify it falls back correctly.
Resolve a missing repo ref and confirm it returns a clear validation failure.

description: Telegram coding-task entry uses explicit entry detection plus a unified slot extractor instead of hardcoded multi-branch sentence parsing.
steps: Feed the extractor explicit Telegram commands using both 开始编程 and /coding.
Verify it extracts the same repo ref and goal from natural expressions like repo 的 goal and repo goal.
Confirm ordinary non-coding chat without an explicit entry does not produce a coding-task request.

description: Telegram private-chat coding-task routing uses the new intent and repo-resolution layers while preserving the existing shared launcher and fallback behavior.
steps: Send a Telegram private-chat explicit coding command with a repo alias in a focused routing test.
Verify nanobot resolves the repo, creates the task, and auto-launches via the shared launcher.
Confirm no new worker-launch path is added outside the existing launcher abstraction.

description: Telegram private-chat coding commands still fall back cleanly when no launcher is available or when launch fails.
steps: Run the Telegram start flow with no launcher wired and verify it still creates a queued task.
Run the Telegram start flow with a failing launcher and verify the task is preserved with a clear failure response.
Confirm these behaviors continue to work with repo alias resolution enabled.

description: Focused tests cover the explicit-entry detector, slot extractor, repo resolver, and Telegram routing behavior.
steps: Run the coding-task router and agent routing test files.
Verify the suite covers alias-map resolution, ~/Documents fallback, explicit-entry parsing, and launch fallback paths.
Confirm the tests remain scoped away from unrelated repo-wide baseline failures.

description: README explains the new explicit-entry plus intent-extraction behavior and the alias-table plus Documents fallback repo resolution model.
steps: Open the updated README coding-task section.
Verify it documents 开始编程 and /coding as explicit triggers with natural goal wording.
Confirm it explains alias-table priority and the ~/Documents fallback.

description: The live nanobot gateway can be restarted in-place and manually exercise a Telegram coding command that uses natural repo-plus-goal phrasing.
steps: Restart the existing nanobot tmux gateway in-place after the refactor.
Send a Telegram private-chat command like 开始编程 codex-remote 底部tab设置icon换一个.
Verify the task enters the coding-task flow instead of falling through to normal chat.
